mnemonics: P.A.S.S. the Probability: P(A) = Sample Space Success. Think of Probability (P) of an event (A) as the ratio of its successful outcomes to all possibilities in the Sample Space (S).
keyPoints: Probability, P(A), is a number between 0 and 1 representing the likelihood of event A.
The Sample Space, S, is the set of all possible outcomes of an experiment.
The probability of an event is calculated as the number of favorable outcomes divided by the total number of outcomes in the sample space, P(A) = |A| / |S|.
commonMistakes: Confusing the probability of an event, P(A), with its complement, P(not A). They sum to 1: P(A) + P(not A) = 1.
Mistaking independent events for mutually exclusive events. Independent events can both occur (P(A and B)=P(A)P(B)), while mutually exclusive events cannot (P(A and B)=0).
